title: quirina microphyla
intro: Quirina microphyla, also known as Quinine Tree or Cinchona, is a flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, native to the Andean region of South America. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its quinine content, which has been used for centuries to treat malaria and other diseases. The health benefits of Quirina microphyla include reducing fever and pain, as well as acting as an antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent. Quirina microphyla can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine as a powder or extract.
